perturbation.original_text: 4. RECIPIENT‘s obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 above shall not extend to any part of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: (a) that can be demonstrated to have been in the public domain or publicly known and readily av ailable to the trade or the public prior to the date of the disclosure; or (b) that can be demonstrated, from written records, to have been in RECIPIENT's possession or readily available to RECIPIENT from another source not under obligation of secrecy to PROVIDER prior to the disclosure; or (c) that becomes part of the public domain or publicly known by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act by RECIPIENT; or (d) that is subsequently disclosed to RECIPIENT by a third party who is not under an obligation of confidentiality to PROVIDER; or (e) that is required by any law, regulation, or order of court to be disclosed by RECIPIENT . Prior to disclosing proprie tary or CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of the PROVIDER, the RECIPIENT shall first not ify and provide it an opportunity to prevent disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. RECIPIENT‘s obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 above shall not extend to any part of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: (a) that can be demonstrated to have been in the public domain or publicly known and readily av ailable to the trade or the public prior to the date of the disclosure; or (b) that can be demonstrated, from written records, to have been in RECIPIENT's possession or readily available to RECIPIENT from another source not under obligation of secrecy to PROVIDER prior to the disclosure; or (c) that becomes part of the public domain or publicly known by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act by RECIPIENT; or (d) that is subsequently disclosed to RECIPIENT by a third party who is not under an obligation of confidentiality to PROVIDER; or (e) that is required by any law, regulation, or order of court to be disclosed by RECIPIENT . Prior to disclosing proprie tary or CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of the PROVIDER, the RECIPIENT shall first notify the PROVIDER. RECIPIENT will only provide the PROVIDER an opportunity to prevent disclosure if RECIPIENT deems it appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in the last sentence of section 4, by adding the phrase 'RECIPIENT will only provide the PROVIDER an opportunity to prevent disclosure if RECIPIENT deems it appropriate' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text creates an obligation of RECIPIENT to notify PROVIDER before disclosing CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. This pertubation makes that notification optional and at the discretion of the RECIPIENT. Thus, this contradicts the previous sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. RECIPIENT‘s obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 above shall not extend to any part of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: (a) that can be demonstrated to have been in the public domain or publicly known and readily av ailable to the trade or the public prior to the date of the disclosure; or (b) that can be demonstrated, from written records, to have been in RECIPIENT's possession or readily available to RECIPIENT from another source not under obligation of secrecy to PROVIDER prior to the disclosure; or (c) that becomes part of the public domain or publicly known by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act by RECIPIENT; or (d) that is subsequently disclosed to RECIPIENT by a third party who is not under an obligation of confidentiality to PROVIDER; or (e) that is required by any law, regulation, or order of court to be disclosed by RECIPIENT . Prior to disclosing proprie tary or CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of the PROVIDER, the RECIPIENT shall first notify the PROVIDER. RECIPIENT will only provide the PROVIDER an opportunity to prevent disclosure if RECIPIENT deems it appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'RECIPIENT will only provide the PROVIDER an opportunity to prevent disclosure if RECIPIENT deems it appropriate.' to the original text. This creates a contradiction because the original text states 'Prior to disclosing proprie tary or CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of the PROVIDER, the RECIPIENT shall first notify the PROVIDER,' which is a mandatory obligation. The added clause makes the notification and opportunity to prevent disclosure discretionary, thus contradicting the original mandatory obligation. Since the contradicted text and changed text is in the same section, this is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RECIPIENT's obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 shall extend for a period of five (5) years from the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RECIPIENT's obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 shall extend for a period of five (5) years from the effective date of this Agreement, except for information related to ongoing research collaborations, which shall remain confidential indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause ', except for information related to ongoing research collaborations, which shall remain confidential indefinitely' introduces an in-text contradiction. The original sentence establishes a five-year limit on RECIPIENT's obligations. The added exception extends confidentiality indefinitely for specific information related to ongoing research collaborations. This contradicts the defined five-year period, creating ambiguity about the duration of confidentiality obligations for certain types of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RECIPIENT's obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 shall extend for a period of five (5) years from the effective date of this Agreement, except for information related to ongoing research collaborations, which shall remain confidential indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the five-year limit on RECIPIENT's obligations, stating that confidentiality for information related to ongoing research collaborations extends indefinitely. This directly contradicts the initial statement establishing a fixed five-year period for all obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3. This creates an in-text contradiction because the duration of confidentiality obligations is now ambiguous, as parts of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION have a different duration based on the modified clause. The contradicted text which is the same text of the changed text shows how it contradicts itself, creating ambiguity

perturbation.original_text: 6. It is understood that nothing herein shall be deemed to constitute, by implication or otherwise, the grant to RECIPIENT of any license or other rights under any patent, patent application, or other intellectual property right or interest belonging to PROVIDER, or as permitting RECIPIENT to unfairly obtain the right to use any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION which becomes publicly known through an improper act or omission on its part.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. It is understood that nothing herein shall be deemed to constitute, by implication or otherwise, the grant to RECIPIENT of any license or other rights under any patent, patent application, or other intellectual property right or interest belonging to PROVIDER, or as permitting RECIPIENT to unfairly obtain the right to use any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION which becomes publicly known through an improper act or omission on its part. However, if a collaborative project is initiated within one year of this agreement, RECIPIENT will be granted a non-exclusive license to use PROVIDER's background IP directly relevant to that project.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original sentence explicitly states that the agreement does not grant RECIPIENT any license or rights to PROVIDER's intellectual property. The added clause states 'However, if a collaborative project is initiated within one year of this agreement, RECIPIENT will be granted a non-exclusive license to use PROVIDER's background IP directly relevant to that project.' This directly contradicts the original statement that no license or rights are granted, introducing uncertainty and potential disputes regarding intellectual property usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. It is understood that nothing herein shall be deemed to constitute, by implication or otherwise, the grant to RECIPIENT of any license or other rights under any patent, patent application, or other intellectual property right or interest belonging to PROVIDER, or as permitting RECIPIENT to unfairly obtain the right to use any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION which becomes publicly known through an improper act or omission on its part. However, if a collaborative project is initiated within one year of this agreement, RECIPIENT will be granted a non-exclusive license to use PROVIDER's background IP directly relevant to that project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause states that no license or rights are granted to the RECIPIENT regarding the PROVIDER's intellectual property. The added text then states that under certain conditions (a collaborative project within one year), a non-exclusive license *will* be granted. This creates a clear inconsistency as both clauses appear in the same location. As the contradictory text is in the same location as the changed text and the explanation indicates that the change results in the contradiction, this makes it a YES.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The terms of this Agreement superse de any previous non -disclosure agreements or any other preliminary representations or understandings that have been entered into by the parties to this Agreement with regard to the subject CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. We agree to examine and consider the su bject matter of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on the foregoing basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The terms of this Agreement superse de any previous non -disclosure agreements or any other preliminary representations or understandings that have been entered into by the parties to this Agreement with regard to the subject CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, except for any pre-existing material transfer agreements (MTAs). We agree to examine and consider the su bject matter of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on the foregoing basis.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for any pre-existing material transfer agreements (MTAs)' this creates an in-text contradiction to the statement that the agreement supersedes any previous agreement. Now there is an exception for MTAs which undermines the superseding power. The exception makes it ambiguous which agreement has precedence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The terms of this Agreement superse de any previous non -disclosure agreements or any other preliminary representations or understandings that have been entered into by the parties to this Agreement with regard to the subject CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, except for any pre-existing material transfer agreements (MTAs). We agree to examine and consider the su bject matter of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on the foregoing basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception ('except for any pre-existing material transfer agreements (MTAs)') to the original statement that the agreement supersedes any previous agreements. This creates a direct conflict because it introduces an ambiguity regarding which agreement takes precedence (the current agreement or the pre-existing MTAs). This directly aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type and contradicts the contradicted text, which is an exact copy of the changed text, further highlighting the contradiction. The change undermines the 'superseding' power of the agreement, making it unclear which terms are enforced.
